title: PhD
biography: Michael Houghton is a British-Canadian virologist who was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 2020 for the discovery of the Hepatitis C virus in 1989. His work enabled blood screening tests that have prevented millions of HCV infections worldwide.
aiSummary: Michael Houghton is a virology specialist with an H-index of 105 at the University of Alberta. Nobel Prize 2020 for discovery of Hepatitis C virus. Based in Edmonton, Canada.
techniquesInvented: Molecular cloning approach for HCV identification

affiliations.role: Li Ka Shing Professor of Virology

affiliations.hospitalName: Li Ka Shing Institute of Virology

citations.title: Isolation of a cDNA clone derived from a blood-borne non-A, non-B viral hepatitis genome
Genetic organization and diversity of the hepatitis C virus
An assay for circulating antibodies to a major etiologic virus of human non-A, non-B hepatitis

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2020)

timeline.title: Identified the Hepatitis C virus
timeline.description: Led team at Chiron Corporation that cloned and identified HCV, ending decades of 'non-A, non-B hepatitis' mystery

timeline.title: Lasker Award
timeline.description: Received the Lasker-DeBakey Clinical Medical Research Award for HCV discovery

timeline.title: Appointed to University of Alberta
timeline.description: Named Li Ka Shing Professor of Virology and Director of the Li Ka Shing Institute

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Shared the Nobel Prize with Harvey Alter and Charles Rice for the discovery of Hepatitis C virus
